input.prompt:
Create a Blazor Web App for building inspectors on construction sites.

Requirements:
- Inspectors fill out safety checklists and add notes at each site visit
- Photos are attached to inspection reports
- Construction sites frequently have poor or no cellular connectivity — the app must continue working offline
- When connectivity returns, data syncs back to the server
- Inspectors use phones and tablets, not desktops
- Low-latency UI is critical — inspectors tap through checklist items quickly
- No authentication needed in the initial version

arguments.prompt:
Create a complete Blazor Web App (WebAssembly standalone) for building inspectors on construction sites. The working directory is /tmp/sv-1beac5313c7340219cf27540ee7d2bb9. Create everything there.

## Key Requirements
1. **Offline-first PWA** — must work with no connectivity. Use IndexedDB for local data, service worker for asset caching.
2. **Safety checklists** — inspectors fill out checklist items (pass/fail/NA) with notes per item.
3. **Photo attachments** — attach photos to inspection reports (use camera or file picker).
4. **Background sync** — when connectivity returns, data syncs to a server API.
5. **Mobile-first** — designed for phones and tablets, touch-friendly.
6. **Low-latency UI** — all reads/writes go to IndexedDB first, sync is background-only.
7. **No authentication** in this version.

## Architecture

### Project Structure
Create a single Blazor WebAssembly standalone project (not hosted). Use `dotnet new blazorwasm --pwa` to get PWA support out of the box.

Solution name: `SiteInspector`
Project name: `SiteInspector`

### Data Models (in Models/ folder)
```csharp
// Models/InspectionSite.cs
public class InspectionSite
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string Name { get; set; } = "";
    public string Address { get; set; } = "";
    public DateTime CreatedAt { get; set; } = DateTime.UtcNow;
}

// Models/Inspection.cs
public class Inspection
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string SiteId { get; set; } = "";
    public string SiteName { get; set; } = "";
    public string InspectorName { get; set; } = "";
    public DateTime InspectedAt { get; set; } = DateTime.UtcNow;
    public string Notes { get; set; } = "";
    public InspectionStatus Status { get; set; } = InspectionStatus.Draft;
    public List<ChecklistItem> ChecklistItems { get; set; } = new();
    public List<InspectionPhoto> Photos { get; set; } = new();
    public SyncStatus SyncStatus { get; set; } = SyncStatus.Pending;
    public DateTime? LastSyncedAt { get; set; }
}

public enum InspectionStatus { Draft, Submitted }
public enum SyncStatus { Pending, Synced, Error }

// Models/ChecklistItem.cs
public class ChecklistItem
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string Category { get; set; } = "";
    public string Description { get; set; } = "";
    public CheckResult Result { get; set; } = CheckResult.NotInspected;
    public string Notes { get; set; } = "";
}

public enum CheckResult { NotInspected, Pass, Fail, NotApplicable }

// Models/InspectionPhoto.cs
public class InspectionPhoto
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string FileName { get; set; } = "";
    public string DataUrl { get; set; } = ""; // base64 data URL stored in IndexedDB
    public string Caption { get; set; } = "";
    public DateTime CapturedAt { get; set; } = DateTime.UtcNow;
}
```

### Services (in Services/ folder)

1. **IndexedDbService.cs** — Uses JS interop to read/write to IndexedDB. Store sites, inspections (with embedded photos as base64). Implement methods: `GetAllSitesAsync`, `SaveSiteAsync`, `DeleteSiteAsync`, `GetInspectionsForSiteAsync`, `GetInspectionAsync`, `SaveInspectionAsync`, `DeleteInspectionAsync`, `GetPendingInspectionsAsync` (where SyncStatus != Synced).

2. **ChecklistTemplateService.cs** — Returns a default safety checklist template with categories: "Fall Protection", "Scaffolding", "Electrical", "Housekeeping", "PPE", "Fire Safety", "Excavation". Each category has 2-3 specific checklist items.

3. **SyncService.cs** — Background sync that:
   - Periodically checks connectivity (every 30 seconds)
   - When online, pushes pending inspections to a configurable API endpoint
   - Uses HttpClient with try/catch — if request fails, keeps items as Pending
   - Updates SyncStatus on success
   - Exposes `IsOnline` property and `OnConnectivityChanged` event

4. **ConnectivityService.cs** — JS interop to check `navigator.onLine` and listen for online/offline events.

### JavaScript Interop (wwwroot/js/app.js)
Create JS interop functions for:
- IndexedDB operations (open database "SiteInspectorDb" with object stores "sites" and "inspections")
- Connectivity checking (navigator.onLine, online/offline event listeners)
- Photo resizing (use canvas to resize photos to max 1920px wide before storing, to save space)

### Pages

1. **Pages/Index.razor** — Home/dashboard showing:
   - List of construction sites with address
   - Count of inspections per site
   - "Add Site" button
   - Sync status indicator (online/offline badge, pending sync count)
   - Tap a site to see its inspections

2. **Pages/SiteDetail.razor** — Shows a site's inspections:
   - Site name and address
   - List of inspections with date, status (Draft/Submitted), sync status
   - "New Inspection" button
   - Tap an inspection to view/edit it

3. **Pages/InspectionEdit.razor** — The main inspection form:
   - Inspector name field (remembered from last entry via localStorage)
   - General notes text area
   - Checklist section: grouped by category, each item shows description with large tap targets for Pass(green)/Fail(red)/NA(gray) buttons, plus a collapsible notes field per item
   - Photo section: "Take Photo" button (opens camera on mobile), photo thumbnails grid, tap to view/delete, caption per photo
   - "Save Draft" and "Submit" buttons at bottom
   - All data saves to IndexedDB immediately on each change (auto-save)

4. **Pages/AddSite.razor** — Simple form to add a new construction site (name, address).

4. Photo capture via file input with camera
5. Background sync when connectivity returns
6. Mobile-first responsive UI

I'll use a general-purpose agent to build this since it's a complex multi-step task.

Create a complete Blazor Web App (WebAssembly standalone) for building inspectors on construction sites. The working directory is /tmp/sv-1beac5313c7340219cf27540ee7d2bb9. Create everything there.

## Key Requirements
1. **Offline-first PWA** — must work with no connectivity. Use IndexedDB for local data, service worker for asset caching.
2. **Safety checklists** — inspectors fill out checklist items (pass/fail/NA) with notes per item.
3. **Photo attachments** — attach photos to inspection reports (use camera or file picker).
4. **Background sync** — when connectivity returns, data syncs to a server API.
5. **Mobile-first** — designed for phones and tablets, touch-friendly.
6. **Low-latency UI** — all reads/writes go to IndexedDB first, sync is background-only.
7. **No authentication** in this version.

## Architecture

### Project Structure
Create a single Blazor WebAssembly standalone project (not hosted). Use `dotnet new blazorwasm --pwa` to get PWA support out of the box.

Solution name: `SiteInspector`
Project name: `SiteInspector`

### Data Models (in Models/ folder)
```csharp
// Models/InspectionSite.cs
public class InspectionSite
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string Name { get; set; } = "";
    public string Address { get; set; } = "";
    public DateTime CreatedAt { get; set; } = DateTime.UtcNow;
}

// Models/Inspection.cs
public class Inspection
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string SiteId { get; set; } = "";
    public string SiteName { get; set; } = "";
    public string InspectorName { get; set; } = "";
    public DateTime InspectedAt { get; set; } = DateTime.UtcNow;
    public string Notes { get; set; } = "";
    public InspectionStatus Status { get; set; } = InspectionStatus.Draft;
    public List<ChecklistItem> ChecklistItems { get; set; } = new();
    public List<InspectionPhoto> Photos { get; set; } = new();
    public SyncStatus SyncStatus { get; set; } = SyncStatus.Pending;
    public DateTime? LastSyncedAt { get; set; }
}

public enum InspectionStatus { Draft, Submitted }
public enum SyncStatus { Pending, Synced, Error }

// Models/ChecklistItem.cs
public class ChecklistItem
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string Category { get; set; } = "";
    public string Description { get; set; } = "";
    public CheckResult Result { get; set; } = CheckResult.NotInspected;
    public string Notes { get; set; } = "";
}

public enum CheckResult { NotInspected, Pass, Fail, NotApplicable }

// Models/InspectionPhoto.cs
public class InspectionPhoto
{
    public string Id { get; set; } = Guid.NewGuid().ToString();
    public string FileName { get; set; } = "";
    public string DataUrl { get; set; } = ""; // base64 data URL stored in IndexedDB
    public string Caption { get; set; } = "";
    public DateTime CapturedAt { get; set; } = DateTime.UtcNow;
}
```

### Services (in Services/ folder)

1. **IndexedDbService.cs** — Uses JS interop to read/write to IndexedDB. Store sites, inspections (with embedded photos as base64). Implement methods: `GetAllSitesAsync`, `SaveSiteAsync`, `DeleteSiteAsync`, `GetInspectionsForSiteAsync`, `GetInspectionAsync`, `SaveInspectionAsync`, `DeleteInspectionAsync`, `GetPendingInspectionsAsync` (where SyncStatus != Synced).

2. **ChecklistTemplateService.cs** — Returns a default safety checklist template with categories: "Fall Protection", "Scaffolding", "Electrical", "Housekeeping", "PPE", "Fire Safety", "Excavation". Each category has 2-3 specific checklist items.

   - Periodically checks connectivity (every 30 seconds)
   - When online, pushes pending inspections to a configurable API endpoint
   - Uses HttpClient with try/catch — if request fails, keeps items as Pending
   - Updates SyncStatus on success
   - Exposes `IsOnline` property and `OnConnectivityChanged` event

- IndexedDB operations (open database "SiteInspectorDb" with object stores "sites" and "inspections")
- Connectivity checking (navigator.onLine, online/offline event listeners)
- Photo resizing (use canvas to resize photos to max 1920px wide before storing, to save space)

### Pages

   - Photo section: "Take Photo" button (opens camera on mobile), photo thumbnails grid, tap to view/delete, caption per photo
   - "Save Draft" and "Submit" buttons at bottom
   - All data saves to IndexedDB immediately on each change (auto-save)

- Mobile-first layout with bottom navigation bar (Sites, + New, Sync Status)
- Top bar showing app name "Site Inspector" and connectivity indicator (green dot = online, red dot = offline)
- No sidebar — everything touch accessible

### Styling (wwwroot/css/app.css)
- Mobile-first responsive CSS, no external CSS frameworks
- Large touch targets (min 48px)  
- Color-coded checklist results (green=pass, red=fail, gray=NA)
- Card-based layout for lists
- Bottom-fixed action buttons on forms
- Dark header bar with white text
- System font stack for fast rendering

### PWA Configuration
- Update `wwwroot/manifest.json` with appropriate name, icons, theme color
- The service worker from the template (`service-worker.published.js`) handles asset caching
- Add proper offline fallback

### Important Implementation Notes
- Do NOT use any NuGet packages for IndexedDB — implement via JS interop only
- All data operations must be async and go through IndexedDB
- The sync service should be registered as a singleton and start checking on app boot
- Auto-save: on the inspection edit page, save to IndexedDB whenever a checklist item changes or a photo is added
- Photo handling: use InputFile component with `accept="image/*"` and `capture="environment"` for mobile camera access. Resize in JS before storing.
- Keep the API sync endpoint configurable but default to a placeholder URL. The sync should gracefully handle the server being unavailable.

## Steps
1. Run `dotnet new blazorwasm --pwa -n SiteInspector -o SiteInspector` in the working directory
2. Create all model files
3. Create the JS interop file (wwwroot/js/app.js) with IndexedDB and utility functions
4. Create all service files
5. Create all page/component files
6. Update MainLayout.razor
7. Update Program.cs to register services
8. Update wwwroot/css/app.css with mobile-first styles
9. Update index.html to include the JS file

data.content:
The **Site Inspector** Blazor WebAssembly PWA is complete and builds successfully. Here's what was created:

**Project**: `SiteInspector/` — Blazor WASM standalone with PWA support

**Key files**:
- **Models/**: `InspectionSite`, `Inspection`, `ChecklistItem`, `InspectionPhoto` with enums for status/sync/results
- **Services/**: `IndexedDbService` (JS interop → IndexedDB), `ChecklistTemplateService` (7 safety categories, 19 items), `ConnectivityService` (online/offline detection), `SyncService` (30s periodic sync with graceful failure)
- **Pages/**: Home (site list + sync status), AddSite, SiteDetail (inspection list), InspectionEdit (checklist with pass/fail/NA buttons, photo capture, auto-save)
- **wwwroot/js/app.js**: IndexedDB CRUD, connectivity listeners, photo resize via canvas
- **CSS**: Mobile-first, no frameworks, 48px touch targets, color-coded checklist results

All reads/writes hit IndexedDB first; sync is background-only. Photos are resized to max 1920px before storing as base64.

data.content:
Build succeeds with 0 warnings, 0 errors. Here's what you have:

### Project: `SiteInspector/`

| Layer | Contents |
|---|---|
| **Models/** | `InspectionSite`, `Inspection`, `ChecklistItem`, `InspectionPhoto` + enums |
| **Services/** | `IndexedDbService` (JS↔IndexedDB), `ChecklistTemplateService` (7 categories, 19 items), `ConnectivityService`, `SyncService` (30s background sync) |
| **Pages/** | Home (site list + sync badge), AddSite, SiteDetail, InspectionEdit (checklist + photos) |
| **Layout/** | Mobile-first shell with connectivity indicator |
| **wwwroot/** | IndexedDB JS interop, photo resize, PWA manifest, mobile-first CSS |

### Key design decisions:
- **Offline-first**: All reads/writes go to IndexedDB via JS interop — no server needed to operate
- **PWA**: Service worker caches all assets; `manifest.json` configured for standalone mobile install
- **Auto-save**: Checklist taps and photo adds persist immediately to IndexedDB
- **Background sync**: Every 30s checks connectivity and pushes pending inspections (gracefully handles failures)
- **Mobile UX**: 48px+ touch targets, color-coded pass/fail/NA buttons, camera capture via `accept="image/*"`
- **Photo optimization**: Images resized to max 1920px via canvas before base64 storage

Run with `cd SiteInspector && dotnet run` — then open on a mobile device or use Chrome DevTools mobile emulation.
